title: Off-road vehicle regulations, proposed : environmental impact statement.

entities.quote: The Izaak Walton League of America suggested a modification of alternative "A" in that the use of off-road vehicles se prohibited except when and where it is expressly permitted. They went further to suggest that the Forest Service temporarily permit the use to continue where it is currently present and not damaging the resource, then after study and public involvement of all areas to modify the temporary permits.
entities.stance_basis: Suggested modified prohibition with temporary permits pending study

entities.quote: The conservation organizations were almost unanimous in their feeling that all off-road vehicle use should be immediately halted pending study
entities.stance_basis: Unanimously favored immediate halt to all ORV use pending study

entities.quote: some off-road vehicle group expressions were that Mational Forests are public lands and therefore no controls should be associated stated it invringed on their constitutional rights.
entities.stance_basis: Argued controls infringe constitutional rights and opposed regulation

entities.quote: Governmental agencies (Federal, State and local) that responded on the alternatives were overwhelmingly in favor of alternative "B" as the logical approach to the control of off-road vehicle use.
entities.stance_basis: Overwhelmingly favored Alternative B for ORV control

entities.entity: Off-Road Vehicle Task Force of the Sierra Club
entities.quote: The Off-Road Vehicle Task Force of the Sierra Club stated that such exemption is unnecessary to the legitimate needs of the miner and drastically weaken the regulation.
entities.stance_basis: Opposed mining exemption as unnecessary and weakening regulations

entities.quote: The Environmental Protection Agency recommended that the regulations incorporate a provision allowing Forest Supervisors and District Rangers discretionary authority to require registration of exempted vehicles.
entities.stance_basis: Recommended adding discretionary registration authority for exempted vehicles

entities.quote: The Environmental Protection Agency felt unless extensive public hearing processes and/or Environmental Statements are anticipated the time frame for implementation should be compressed to a year from the date of issuance of the regulations in order that all Forest System lands that should be closed to ORV use in order to protect the environment are quickly protected.
entities.stance_basis: Recommended compressing designation timeline to one year

entities.quote: Oil interests, as well as mining interests, were in favor of the exemption
entities.stance_basis: Supported mining/mineral exemption from ORV regulations

entities.quote: Oil interests, as well as mining interests, were in favor of the exemption
entities.stance_basis: Supported mining exemption from ORV regulations

entities.quote: Texaco Inc. also recommended exemption for oil and gas exploration and operations under the Mineral Leasing Laws.
entities.stance_basis: Recommended extending exemption to oil and gas exploration

entities.role: federal agency commenter, bureau of Department of the Interior
entities.quote: We therefore urgently recommend that your proposed regulation be amended to exempt Federal agencies, their contractors and employees from the necessity of securing prior express approval from the Chief, Forest Service, in the use of off-road vehicles.
entities.stance_basis: Recommended exemption for federal agencies and contractors from permit requirement

entities.entity: Bureau of Indian Affairs, Albuquerque Area Office
entities.quote: The impact statement leaves much to be desired. The broad scope of the subject is appreciated, however, the approach to the entire problem, is in effect, to remove or restrict.
entities.stance_basis: Criticized EIS as inadequate and biased, raised concerns without clear stance on regulations

entities.entity: Department of the Army, South Pacific Division, Corps of Engineers
entities.quote: The proposed plan does not conflict with existing or authorized plans of the Corps of Engineers. Recognizing that such aspects are not a primary responsibility of the Corps of Engineers, we would, however, like to make the following general comments and suggestions:
entities.stance_basis: No conflict found; offered procedural suggestions without opposing or supporting

entities.entity: Department of the Army, Fort Leonard Wood
entities.quote: Your proposed regulations are essentially the same as we have discussed with Clark National Forest personnel and have our concurrence.
entities.stance_basis: Explicitly concurred with proposed regulations

entities.quote: The only comment I have is to suggest adding "vehicles of state and federal agencies" to the first paragraph under section IV C. This would permit various agencies to use off-road vehicles for the purpose of conducting scientific studies in a National Forest.
entities.stance_basis: Suggested adding exemption for state and federal agency vehicles for scientific studies

entities.entity: Soil Conservation Service, USDA, Reno, Nevada
entities.quote: If our problem of ingress and egress can be handled by permit, lease, license, or contract from the Chief of the Forest Service, it will satisfy our needs.
entities.stance_basis: Conditionally satisfied if permit system accommodates snow survey access needs

entities.entity: Soil Conservation Service, USDA, Washington, D.C.
entities.quote: We are in agreement with the need to limit the use of off-road vehicles. We have had a number of recent experiences where irresponsible snowmobilers have used snow courses for drag strips with a resultant loss of valuable snow data. We do feel, however, that where a resource management program such as snow surveys is involved, it would be desirable to have a simpler system than outlined in the amendment for obtaining authorization to use off-road vehicles in prohibited areas.
entities.stance_basis: Agreed with need for limits but sought simpler permit process for snow survey programs

entities.entity: Soil Conservation Service, USDA, Albuquerque, New Mexico
entities.quote: It is our opinion that alternative B will provide for the orderly designation and development of the needed ORV activity areas on National Forests.
entities.stance_basis: Supported Alternative B as providing orderly ORV designation

entities.quote: Since the Commission has no programmatic interests affected by the regulations and administrative instructions nor any special expertise by law for evaluating the environmental impact of the statement, the statement has not been reviewed and no comments are offered.
entities.stance_basis: Declined to review or comment; no programmatic interest

entities.quote: Numerous conservation groups did not specifically address either of the alternatives but dealt with the draft Environmental Statement as a whole. These groups generally felt that the Statement was inadequate in that it dealt in generalities rather than specifics and that many of the impacts are discussed by conclusionary statements.
entities.stance_basis: Criticized EIS as inadequate and too general without taking a stance on alternatives

entities.quote: Respondents to this issue were of the majority opinion that open areas should be posted rather than the closed areas.
entities.stance_basis: Preferred signing open areas rather than closed areas for ORV use

entities.quote: The majority of respondents addressing this issue were not in favor of this exemption.
entities.stance_basis: Majority opposed the mining exemption from ORV regulations

entities.quote: A majority of the responses that discussed this issue favored alternative "B" as the course of action to be followed.
entities.stance_basis: Majority favored Alternative B (continued recognition with controls)

entities.quote: We therefore urgently recommend that your proposed regulation be amended to exempt Federal agencies, their contractors and employees from the necessity of securing prior express approval from the Chief, Forest Service, in the use of off-road vehicles.
entities.stance_basis: Signed letter recommending federal agency exemption from permit requirement

entities.role: Colonel, CE, Facilities Engineer, Fort Leonard Wood
entities.quote: Your proposed regulations are essentially the same as we have discussed with Clark National Forest personnel and have our concurrence.
entities.stance_basis: Signed letter explicitly concurring with proposed regulations

entities.quote: The only comment I have is to suggest adding "vehicles of state and federal agencies" to the first paragraph under section IV C.
entities.stance_basis: Signed letter suggesting exemption for state and federal agency vehicles

entities.role: Administrator, Soil Conservation Service, Washington D.C.
entities.quote: We are in agreement with the need to limit the use of off-road vehicles. We have had a number of recent experiences where irresponsible snowmobilers have used snow courses for drag strips with a resultant loss of valuable snow data. We do feel, however, that where a resource management program such as snow surveys is involved, it would be desirable to have a simpler system than outlined in the amendment for obtaining authorization to use off-road vehicles in prohibited areas.
entities.stance_basis: Agreed with ORV limits but sought simpler authorization for snow survey programs

entities.role: State Conservationist, Soil Conservation Service, Albuquerque
entities.quote: It is our opinion that alternative B will provide for the orderly designation and development of the needed ORV activity areas on National Forests.
entities.stance_basis: Signed letter supporting Alternative B

entities.role: Director, Division of Environmental Affairs, U.S. Atomic Energy Commission
entities.quote: Since the Commission has no programmatic interests affected by the regulations and administrative instructions nor any special expertise by law for evaluating the environmental impact of the statement, the statement has not been reviewed and no comments are offered.
entities.stance_basis: Signed letter declining to comment due to no programmatic interest

entities.quote: Generally, we feel that the environmental statement should be a comprehensive and objective statement of the environmental impact of the proposed action rather than a justification for the action, and that the preparation of the statement should include input, as appropriate, from other public agencies significantly concerned.
entities.stance_basis: Criticized EIS as justification rather than objective analysis; raised procedural concerns
